game_name: Broken Sword: The Shadow of the Templars

design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect items, combine them, use dialogue trees with NPCs to obtain clues, solve environmental puzzles to progress through a globe-trotting conspiracy narrative
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Unraveling a Knights Templar conspiracy while being pursued by Khan (the assassin) and competing factions seeking the same ancient treasures
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a clever amateur detective/tourist uncovering historical secrets across European and Middle Eastern locations; solving puzzles through observation and deduction rather than action
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Narrative-first design: story prominence rivals (or exceeds) puzzle complexity; serious tone anchored in real Knights Templar research but balanced with humor; death is possible (unusual for adventure games at the time), creating consequence; dialogue-driven world-building through conversation icons rather than exposition cutscenes; puzzle solutions require careful environmental observation and NPC interaction rather than trial-and-error

difficulty.lives_system: Single life; protagonist death is possible, player restarts from last save point
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save points; player can load from last save after death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls pacing through dialogue selection and puzzle-solving approach; save system provides safety net while maintaining consequence for mistakes

progression.upgrade_system: Inventory-based progression; player accumulates items that unlock new puzzle solutions and story revelations; no traditional RPG upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated locations unlock as manuscript clues are discovered; new areas added to map as narrative unfolds; item combinations enable access to previously inaccessible areas and NPCs
replayability_hooks: Multiple dialogue tree paths with NPCs provide alternative conversations
Hidden clues and environmental details reward close observation
Director's Cut (2009-2012) expanded content provides alternate version

world_building_techniques: Dialogue trees through conversation icons with NPCs revealing plot and background
Environmental interaction and item examination providing contextual clues
Animated environments in style of classic animated films adding narrative tone
Historical research on Knights Templar integrated into plot structure
Multiple locations across Europe and Middle East creating geographic narrative scope

boss_design.philosophy: Primary antagonist Khan pursues player throughout narrative as recurring threat rather than discrete boss encounters; conflict is investigative/evasive rather than combat-based
notable_bosses: Khan (the assassin): recurring antagonist who appears in different costumes/disguises, creating pattern-recognition challenge for player to identify him; represents the physical manifestation of the conspiracy

faction_indicators: Knights Templar conspiracy faction (pursuing the treasure)
danger_signals: Khan's repeated appearances in disguise signal escalating pursuit
Assassinations of individuals connected to the manuscript indicate conspiracy scope
Theft attempts of the tripod and gem indicate multiple parties seeking the same treasure
environmental_storytelling: Café de la chandelle verte as initial crime scene establishing conspiracy
Hotel safe containing ancient manuscript as evidence of systematic treasure hunt
Archaeological excavation site at Lochmarne revealing historical context
Montfauçon location referenced in mural providing geographic puzzle progression
Museum housing tripod connecting present-day institutions to historical secrets

sources_used: wikipedia: Broken Sword - The Shadow of the Templars
